sanskrit: नियतं कुरु कर्म त्वं कर्म ज्यायो ह्यकर्मणः । शरीरयात्रापि च ते न प्रसिद्ध्येदकर्मणः ॥
hindiTranslation: तू शास्त्रविधिसे नियत किये हुए कर्तव्यकर्म कर क्योंकि कर्म न करनेकी अपेक्षा कर्म करना श्रेष्ठ है तथा कर्म न करनेसे तेरा शरीरनिर्वाह भी सिद्ध नहीं होगा ॥
englishTranslation: Do the duty prescribed for you. Action is better than inaction, and even your body cannot be sustained by inaction.
essence: Right action is unavoidable; even survival depends on it.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna tells Arjuna to do the duty assigned to him. Action is better than sitting idle, and even basic life cannot continue through inaction.
simpleInsight: There is relief in moving forward: doing the needed thing is cleaner than freezing in fear.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is paralysed with doubt. Krishna answers the paralysis directly: do the duty that is already yours. This verse turns the argument from fear into action.
modernRelevance: You are staring at the unanswered message, the apology, the hard resignation email. Waiting for perfect certainty only deepens the knot; action is what loosens it.
translationLiteral: नियतं (prescribed) / कुरु (do) / कर्म (action, duty) / त्वं (you) / कर्म (action) / ज्यायः (better) / हि (indeed) / अकर्मणः (than inaction) / शरीरयात्रा (bodily maintenance, livelihood) / अपि (also, even) / च (and) / ते (your) / न (not) / प्रसिद्ध्येत् (would be accomplished) / अकर्मणः (by inaction)
